description: average_job_cost_cents: denominator changed from count-of-done jobs to len(jobs) (all jobs, incl. pending/failed with no cost), permanently under-reporting the average
is_stale: now_ms (a millisecond delta) is compared directly against STALE_AFTER_SECONDS (still a seconds constant), so staleness triggers ~1000x too eagerly
has_capacity: boundary changed from `<` to `<=`, so one job beyond max_concurrency can still be dispatched (off-by-one)
apply_discount: total is recomputed as subtotal + tax instead of the already-discounted total + tax, silently dropping the discount from the final total
primary_label: job.tags[0] raises TypeError now that api.submit_job passes tags=None straight through instead of coercing it to a list

invoice_summary: `if not entry.amount_cents` still treats zero-cost entries as placeholders and skips them, now silently excluding the legitimate zero-cost charges MIN_CHARGE_CENTS=0 was meant to allow
mark_done never deletes the job's entry from self._leases, so the lease dict (written on every dispatch) grows without bound
record_charge dropped its try/except retry-once on store.write, but nothing wraps the store in RetryingStore, so a transient write failure now raises instead of retrying
cancel_job sets status "cancelled" but STATUS_LABELS (unchanged) has no entry for it, so the API returns the raw status string instead of a human label like every other status
get_job_duration_ms does job.metadata["started_at_ms"] without checking job.metadata is None, which it is for any job that hasn't been dispatched yet
